memory.summary: The assignment can wait; hanging out with cohort_3 feels way more important right now than finishing that reading.
Everyone is finally here and ordering food! I'm so relieved we didn't miss out, even if the assignment is still sitting there.
My stomach is rumbling and I'm starving, but at least we're all together now. The assignment can wait; this food with the crew is what matters most right now.
I let the moment pass again, and honestly, that's fine; the crew is still here eating. The assignment can wait until later, I just don't want to ruin the vibe now.

Nothing changed again, but the crew is still laughing and I'm not stressing about it. The assignment can wait; right now, just being here with them feels enough.
I'm still ignoring the assignment to keep things light with the crew, even though I'm a bit tired. The vibe is too good to ruin right now.
I let the moment pass again, and honestly, that's fine; the crew is still here eating. The assignment can wait until later, I just don't want to ruin the vibe now.
I finally told the crew I'm stuck in the library, but honestly, I just wanted to get back to them without any awkwardness.
I'm finally in the zone, but I still feel like I need to check in with the crew so they don't think I ghosted them.
I promised to grab food with the crew, but my energy is dropping and I'm too stressed to actually follow through. I just hope they don't think I ghosted them again.
I missed the deadline while trying to keep everyone happy, and now I'm stressed but too drained to fix it.

goals.description: Immediately message cohort_3 to apologize for the late reply, blame a sudden 'tech glitch' or 'family emergency' (avoiding the truth), and pivot quickly to planning a make-up hangout this weekend.

The avoidant trait makes me terrified of explaining that I just procrastinated; lying about a technical issue feels safer than admitting failure. I need to secure the relationship bond immediately to stop the guilt from overwhelming me.
